title: Last value of readmemh is not correctly read when no newline
body:
Hi!

I'm noticing a bit of unexpected behaviour where the last value of a memory dump file is not read in correctly:

Contents of `error.sv`:
```systemverilog
module error();
    reg [7:0] rom [4];
    initial begin
        $dumpfile("waveform.vcd");
        $dumpvars;
        $readmemh("rom_image.mem", rom);
        #10;
    end
endmodule
```

Contents of `rom_image.mem` _note that there's **no newline** at the end of the file._
```
00
10
20
30
``` 

![image](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/24391306/311c0299-88c8-4341-a159-8180de87a316)

If I **add a newline** at the end of the file (which I probably should have done in the first place anyway, but that is not the question)
It is handled correctly:

![Screenshot from 2024-03-25 14-21-46](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/24391306/04b78e69-154d-4398-a089-092db49bde1b)

At least a warning would have been nice here :smile:

> What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
```
verilator --binary --trace error.sv
```
> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
```
Verilator 5.022 2024-02-24 rev UNKNOWN.REV
```
> What OS and distribution are you using?
```
Arch Linux 6.6.22-1-lts
```

Best regards,

Josse
